route_inventory.transaction: one project-scoped read connection per repository call
route_inventory.revision_binding: deliverable updated_at per item; parity compares canonical JSON
proposal updated_at per item; parity compares canonical JSON
proposal updated_at; parity compares canonical JSON

route_inventory.transaction: bounded project-scoped reads for deliverable, milestones and links
route_inventory.revision_binding: deliverable updated_at plus linked task snapshots; parity compares canonical JSON

route_inventory.transaction: bounded project-scoped reads; process-local TTL cache keyed by mission cache stamp
route_inventory.revision_binding: deliverable updated_at plus every linked project's task stamp; response ETag

route_inventory.transaction: bounded project-scoped reads; process-local TTL cache keyed by mission cache stamp
route_inventory.revision_binding: deliverable updated_at plus every linked project's task stamp; response ETag

route_inventory.transaction: bounded project-scoped reads; process-local TTL cache
route_inventory.revision_binding: deliverable updated_at plus linked task state; response ETag

route_inventory.transaction: one committed project-scoped metadata read; never computes or records closure
route_inventory.revision_binding: immutable report_id and evidence_hash, retained history, canonical JSON parity

writer_inventory.transaction: one project _write_through transaction atomically upserts the deliverable and activity; narration enqueue is post-commit and idempotent
one project connection transaction validates and updates supplied contract fields and writes the deliverable.updated activity; narration enqueue is post-commit
one project connection transaction upserts the milestone, activity, and parent updated_at; narration enqueue is post-commit
one project _write_through transaction writes the link, activity, and parent updated_at; optional dependency closure is explicit post-commit orchestration
one project connection transaction deletes the link, writes activity, and touches the parent; narration enqueue is post-commit
grading precedes one project _write_through transaction that atomically persists report metadata and deliverable.closure_verified activity
monolith orchestration owns separately audited message and wake effects; no claim of one cross-effect SQLite transaction
monolith orchestration performs bounded repository writes as separate committed actions; the whole tick is not one transaction
bounded project-scoped reads return the deliverable existence check and current durable scope rows; excluded from the read-only sidecar surface
one project connection transaction atomically starts or controls the deliverable scope, supersedes covered task scopes when needed, and writes activity
one project connection transaction atomically starts or controls the exact task scope and writes activity; overlap checks share that transaction
brief generation precedes one project metadata/activity transaction; external generation is outside the SQLite transaction
one project connection transaction updates narrative metadata and activity
one project connection transaction supersedes prior proposals, inserts the proposal, and writes activity
monolith-owned multi-project orchestration composes separate milestone, task, link, and proposal-state transactions; it is intentionally not globally atomic
draft generation precedes the single project proposal transaction; optional LLM work is outside SQLite
one project connection transaction updates deliverable status and updated_at
one project connection transaction updates proposal payload/outcome and writes activity
one project connection transaction marks the proposal rejected and writes activity
one project connection transaction marks the proposal deferred and writes activity

writer_policy.closure_rule: verify, grade, deliverable metadata update and deliverable.closure_verified activity stamp remain one monolith-owned write transaction
writer_policy.reader_rule: the service may expose only committed closure reports; it may not compute, request, grade or persist closure

auth_project_scope.current_monolith_boundary: global API middleware; current handlers do not bind a read principal directly
auth_project_scope.standalone_rule: ARCH-MS-110 must authorize every route through the injected project-scoped read port and must reject missing or cross-project bearer tokens

revision_drift_binding.mission_cache_stamp: deliverables.updated_at plus linked project task stamps
revision_drift_binding.side_by_side_rule: ARCH-MS-110 compares status, body and ETag/canonical payload at the same observed source revision and reports drift instead of masking it
revision_drift_binding.cache_rule: standalone caches are process-local and keyed by the same repository revision stamps

sqlite_contention.model: one monolith closure writer plus three standalone read workers

resource_budget.measurement_scope: live post-Coord host with projectplanner, Auth, Tasks and Coord active
resource_budget.projection_basis: 128 MiB hard cap for a read-only uvicorn, above the live Tasks and Coord RSS values

G1_route_repository_transaction_inventor.reason: All 27 router routes are classified: eight day-one service reads and nineteen monolith-owned routes, each with repository calls and an explicit transaction or orchestration boundary.

G2_closure_transaction_boundary.reason: Closure report persistence and its audit stamp share one SQLite transaction and remain on the monolith.

G3_auth_project_scope.reason: The build contract requires explicit project resolution and a handler-level project-scoped read Auth port on every standalone route.

G4_revision_drift_binding.reason: Mission caches retain repository stamps; closure identity retains report_id/evidence_hash; parity is revision-aware.

G5_sqlite_contention.reason: The executable WAL probe requires atomic snapshots, zero lock errors and quick_check=ok with one writer and three readers.

G6_resource_budget.reason: A 128 MiB cap leaves 582512640 bytes available, 45641728 bytes above the 512 MiB reserve on the live post-Coord host.

go_exit.next_action: Build the port-bound read-only service and prove exact side-by-side parity on :8124; production cut remains a later gated task.
